summary: Test suite has critical structural issues: (1) Tests don't directly test the exported implementation functions (generateTags, sanitizeTag); instead they rely entirely on a workflow that isn't provided, making the tests essentially untestable as written. (2) No unit tests with mocks/fixtures for the core logic—all tests assume workflow exists and runs hardcoded scenarios. (3) Tests reference specific git SHAs (abc1234, def4567) but the temp repo setup never creates commits with these values. (4) No error condition testing (missing ref, invalid SHA, etc.) despite implementation having error handling. (5) Tests violate TDD requirement—written after implementation with no 'failing test first' pattern. (6) Single act run in beforeAll means all string-matching tests are interdependent and brittle; if workflow output format changes, many tests fail simultaneously. (7) String matching is weak (`.toContain('latest')`) with no verification of context or exact output format. Suite would not catch most regressions in the implementation's core logic.
